jokes.text: They devalued the broknik and the royal family is vacationing in Calcutta
jokes.setup: Latka is listening to news from his home country
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of completely made-up currency and nonsensical royal vacation spot
jokes.explanation: Sets up the absurd world-building of Latka's fictional country with deadpan delivery

jokes.text: Ippi Dongpres just set the new indoor pole-vaulting record for my country. He jumped 12 feet.
jokes.setup: Exciting sports news about a pole-vaulting record
jokes.punchline: 12 feet is terrible for pole vaulting
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff with absurd name and disappointing athletic achievement

jokes.text: Yes, but remember the National Sports Arena has a ten-foot ceiling.
jokes.setup: Someone points out 12 feet isn't a good pole vault
jokes.punchline: The venue has impossible architectural constraints
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation - turns a weak record into an impossible achievement through absurd logic

jokes.text: a farmer made a mistake in the dark and tried to milk a billy goat
jokes.setup: On the lighter side news story
jokes.punchline: Farmer's embarrassing barnyard error
jokes.explanation: Simple physical comedy setup, fairly predictable but fits the world-building

jokes.text: Top of the morning! / It's 5:00 p.m., clam brain.
jokes.setup: Someone cheerfully greets with morning phrase
jokes.punchline: Louie's harsh correction pointing out it's evening
jokes.explanation: Classic Louie - turning a friendly greeting into an insult. Good character integration.

jokes.text: I think the boss is starting to warm up to me.
jokes.setup: Louie just called him 'clam brain'
jokes.punchline: Character interprets the insult as progress
jokes.explanation: Good ironic response - shows character's optimism in the face of Louie's abuse

jokes.text: It's okay, Louie? / You bet, Latka. But this counts as your vacation.
jokes.setup: Latka needs to go home due to revolution emergency
jokes.punchline: Louie shows 'compassion' but still docks his vacation time
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie moment - shows his character's selfishness even in a crisis moment

jokes.text: I might as well have been talking to my wrench.
jokes.setup: Character gives long, unhelpful advice to Latka
jokes.punchline: Self-aware admission that the advice was completely useless
jokes.explanation: Good self-deprecating acknowledgment of giving circular, meaningless advice

jokes.text: Somebody shut the doors or this place is going to be crawling with flies.
jokes.setup: Foreign visitor enters speaking broken English
jokes.punchline: Louie's characteristically rude xenophobic comment
jokes.explanation: Typical Louie insult - cruel but consistent with his character

jokes.text: I saw a couple of birds do that on Marlin Perkins once.
jokes.setup: Witnessing the strange greeting ritual
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to animal mating behavior from nature documentary
jokes.explanation: Sharp observational humor connecting cultural ritual to animal behavior

jokes.text: He asks, 'Have you read a good book lately?' / I can see the love of literature in his eyes.
jokes.setup: Baschi says something in foreign language
jokes.punchline: Latka's obvious lie about what he said vs. the sarcastic response
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection where translation clearly doesn't match what was said

jokes.text: Who was in that one? / Me, Sid and 12 illegal aliens. / It was a real carwash.
jokes.setup: Baschi mentions working on 'Sid's Carwash' as if it's a movie
jokes.punchline: Reveals it was actually just a job at a carwash
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - sets up expectation of movie career, delivers mundane reality

jokes.text: What, oh, sure, you are an American on the outside, with all your fine clothes.
jokes.setup: Baschi accuses Latka of being Americanized
jokes.punchline: Reference to Latka's presumably terrible outfit as 'fine clothes'
jokes.explanation: Visual gag based on Latka's characteristically bad fashion sense

jokes.text: ♪ Ibeeda ibee-dorfnish, ibeeda ♪
jokes.setup: Baschi starts singing to persuade Latka
jokes.punchline: Completely made-up language song
jokes.explanation: Absurdist musical moment extending the fictional language world-building

jokes.text: And also because I am a general.
jokes.setup: Latka explains why he must go fight
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation that he holds high military rank
jokes.explanation: Great escalation - turns personal duty into professional obligation with absurd revelation

jokes.text: Yeah, but think how he would treat me if I weren't [nice].
jokes.setup: Someone questions why Latka is nice to Louie despite abuse
jokes.punchline: Latka's logical conclusion that it could be worse
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Latka's practical wisdom about dealing with Louie

jokes.text: Well, I have to go rotate. / You have to circulate.
jokes.setup: Latka uses wrong word for mingling at party
jokes.punchline: Gentle correction of his vocabulary mistake
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay based on Latka's language difficulties

jokes.text: What happened to Chuck and Matt? / I don't want to talk about it. / They didn't make it, huh? / No, they made it. A year later, they married each other. I heard they opened a roller-disco in L.A.
jokes.setup: Tony's emotional war story about buddies who wouldn't all survive
jokes.punchline: They survived but became a gay couple running a business
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - builds dramatic tension then completely subverts with absurd outcome

jokes.text: 'Latka, tonight is your last night here. Tomorrow you go to war, so tonight I will give you my body to use as you will so that you will never forget me.' / No, I wasn't going to say that. / But how about it?
jokes.setup: Latka anticipates what Elaine will offer him
jokes.punchline: Wildly inappropriate sexual proposition followed by her rejection and his persistence
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Latka's inappropriate romantic fantasies

jokes.text: Oh, it is easy to be a general in my country. The hard part is to be a living general.
jokes.setup: Someone comments on Latka becoming a general at young age
jokes.punchline: Reveals that generals have high mortality rate
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor with perfect economy - explains both promotion ease and war danger

jokes.text: The line forms behind me.
jokes.setup: Someone tastes terrible foreign liquor
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic comment about everyone wanting to try it
jokes.explanation: Standard reaction to bad alcohol - functional but not particularly creative

jokes.text: In the old country, they do it like this.
jokes.setup: Traditional drinking custom explanation
jokes.punchline: Presumably involves dramatic physical demonstration
jokes.explanation: Visual gag involving cultural drinking ritual - builds world consistency

jokes.text: Now, this is the part where we all strip to the waist and set fire to the barns in the village.
jokes.setup: Traditional celebration continues
jokes.punchline: Reveals increasingly destructive cultural customs
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of absurd cultural practices from harmless to destructive

jokes.text: We'll fight this thing together. I haven't had a chance to prove it, but I'm as good American as any man. / No, no, Jim, Jim, America is not involved in this one. / It is now.
jokes.setup: Jim volunteers to fight in the revolution
jokes.punchline: Declares unilateral American involvement in foreign conflict
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Jim's well-meaning but misguided patriotism

jokes.text: No, no, Jim, Jim, America is not involved in this one. / It is now.
jokes.setup: Jim volunteering to fight with Latka
jokes.punchline: Jim's declaration unilaterally involving America in foreign conflict
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jim moment - his spaced-out confidence in declaring war. Great character integration and economy.

jokes.text: Death to the puppet regime! / But there is no puppet regime. / Who the hell are we fighting? / The tyrannical despot.
jokes.setup: Jim enthusiastically declares war on various enemies
jokes.punchline: Confusion about who the actual enemy is
jokes.explanation: Good escalating confusion as Jim declares war on non-existent enemies

jokes.text: It's bad enough you're leaving the company, but you're running out on America, too.
jokes.setup: Louie gives Latka severance pay
jokes.punchline: Accuses foreign-born Latka of abandoning America
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Louie's ability to make everything about his own grievances

jokes.text: Louie giving Latka money and telling him to take care of himself after publicly berating him
jokes.setup: Louie publicly refuses to attend the party and insults Latka
jokes.punchline: Privately shows genuine care and affection
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment revealing Louie's hidden emotional depth beneath cruel exterior

jokes.text: Why don't you come and join the party? / No, no. / Hey, Louie... you'd make us all feel miserable if you did. / Okay.
jokes.setup: Polite invitation to Louie to join the party
jokes.punchline: Reverse psychology using Louie's awful personality as motivation
jokes.explanation: Excellent reverse psychology joke that perfectly captures everyone's relationship with Louie

jokes.text: That was from Anthony and Cleopatra. / Oh. Well, thank them both for me.
jokes.setup: Bobby quotes Shakespeare's 'Anthony and Cleopatra'
jokes.punchline: Latka thinks they're contemporary people who should be thanked
jokes.explanation: Good character-based misunderstanding showing Latka's cultural gaps

jokes.text: Visual revelation of the tactical situation on the map
jokes.setup: Map shows rebel stronghold and government forces
jokes.punchline: Visual reveals impossible military odds - two rebels vs. entire army
jokes.explanation: Masterful visual gag that completely recontextualizes the entire episode - the 'revolution' is just two guys

jokes.text: No, no. Now is time for you to kiss my yaktabe.
jokes.setup: Baschi insists they must still go fight despite impossible odds
jokes.punchline: Cultural insult delivered in made-up language
jokes.explanation: Good escalation - Baschi's stubborn insistence despite obvious futility

jokes.text: I'm going home to watch the Tonight Show.
jokes.setup: After rejecting the revolution and Baschi
jokes.punchline: Completely mundane American evening activity
jokes.explanation: Perfect character conclusion - chooses American normalcy over homeland heroics

jokes.text: I'm going home to watch the Tonight Show.
jokes.setup: Epic farewell for revolutionary war
jokes.punchline: Completely mundane American evening plan
jokes.explanation: Perfect deflation of the entire episode's drama - from revolutionary general to American couch potato in one line

jokes.text: Latka singing 'Yankee Doodle Dandy' mixed with his native language
jokes.setup: Latka's final declaration of American identity
jokes.punchline: Blends patriotic American song with foreign language
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Latka's hybrid identity - American but retaining heritage
